headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Samoa (rotation backfill, never assessed). Note canonical countries.json shows Samoa at rank 50 (scanner says 51 — close-rank discrepancy; canonical 50.0 composite used). Published 50.0 / Functional sustained against compound evidence: (1) 2021 election of Fiame Naomi Mata'afa as first female Prime Minister; post-2021-constitutional-crisis governance stabilised; (2) Active climate-vulnerability framing — Samoa committed to 100% renewable electrification by 2030 and decarbonisation of land and maritime transport; Pacific COP solidarity offered to Australia 2026 bid; (3) UNGA national-statement engagement on climate, SDGs, women's leadership; CHOGM 2024 host (positive multilateral-leadership signal); (4) Women's parliamentary representation: 6 of 52 seats — sustained gender-representation gap despite first-female-PM; (5) People-centred development as declared policy focus; protection-of-vulnerable-citizens framing in international fora; (6) Pacific Islands Forum participation; ADB knowledge-events engagement. Dimensional profile (AWR 3, EMP 3, ACT 3, EQU 2.5, BND 3.5, ACC 3, SYS 3, INT 3) consistent with functional-band Pacific-island state with active climate-leadership and democratic consolidation post-2021 crisis. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 50.0 / Functional with first-baseline rationale; Medium confidence.
rank_discrepancy_note: Scanner lists Samoa at rank 51; canonical countries.json shows rank 50. Composite 50.0 sustained per canonical data.
key_evidence: Fiame Naomi Mata'afa: elected 2021 as Samoa's first female Prime Minister; ended 40-year continuous government; post-2021-constitutional-crisis governance stabilised; ongoing leadership through 2026.

Climate commitments: Samoa committed to 100% electrification through renewables by 2030; urgent and inclusive transformation of land and maritime transport sectors toward decarbonisation declared at UNGA.
Pacific COP 2026 solidarity: Samoa among Pacific bloc supporting Australia's COP 2026 bid — sustained multilateral climate-engagement.
CHOGM 2024 host: Samoa hosted Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting October 2024 — significant multilateral-leadership signal.
Women's parliamentary representation: only 6 of 52 Samoan parliament seats held by women — sustained gender-representation gap despite first-female-PM context.

PM Mata'afa and Commonwealth Secretary-General joint position: women's leadership necessity for sustainable and equitable future; encouraged legally enforced quotas and appointments for women's political leadership.
People-centred development framing: government declared focus on safeguarding rights of all citizens 'especially the most vulnerable' through strengthening social protection.
Pacific Islands Forum and SPC (Pacific Community) participation; UN-system engagement active; ADB development-knowledge engagement.
Samoa today described as 'peaceful and stable' despite 2021 constitutional crisis legacy.

evidence.source: UN SDGs — Fiame Naomi Mata'afa Panelist Profile
evidence.finding: First female PM context; UN SDG engagement; multilateral leadership profile.

evidence.source: Pacific Islands Forum Secretariat — Samoa PM Fiame UNGA77 National Statement
evidence.finding: Climate-vulnerability framing; 100% renewable electrification by 2030 commitment; decarbonisation of transport.

evidence.source: Pacific Community SPC — Her Excellency Fiame Naomi Mata'afa
evidence.finding: Multilateral Pacific-leadership profile; people-centred-development framing; vulnerable-citizen protection.

evidence.source: Commonwealth — Samoan PM and Commonwealth Secretary-General on women's leadership
evidence.finding: Women's leadership quotas advocacy; women's parliamentary representation gap (6/52) documented.
CHOGM 2024 host; multilateral-leadership signal; Pacific climate-vulnerability framing.

evidence.source: Lowy Institute — 2023 FDC Pacific Lecture (Fiame Mata'afa)
evidence.finding: Samoa described as peaceful and stable; resilience-and-engagement framing.

evidence.source: Taylor & Francis — Gender and Political Leadership in Samoa: Mata'afa and 2021 Election
evidence.finding: 2021 election scholarly analysis; gender-and-political-leadership context; constitutional-crisis resolution.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. Active Pacific Islands Forum climate-vulnerability monitoring; UN SDG engagement; people-centred-development framing demonstrates suffering-detection structural capacity. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained. Vulnerable-citizen-protection framing in international fora; CHOGM and UNGA affective acknowledgment of Pacific climate-and-development concerns. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained. 100% renewable electrification by 2030 commitment; CHOGM 2024 multilateral-leadership operational; social protection programs functional. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. Women's parliamentary representation (6/52) sustained gender-representation gap despite first-female-PM; matai (chiefly) governance structure has historic gender-asymmetry context. Anchor 2-3.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 sustained. Post-2021-constitutional-crisis governance stabilised; constitutional democracy boundary-of-state-protection clear; multilateral-respecting boundary practice via PIF/CHOGM. Anchor 3-4.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. Multi-party democracy; press freedom regional comparator; CHOGM 2024 multilateral accountability signal. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained. PIF, CHOGM, ADB, SPC, UN multilateral scaffolding; post-2021-crisis constitutional consolidation; first-female-PM democratic-transition operational. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained. Declared climate-leadership, women's-leadership-quotas, and people-centred-development commitments consistent with operational reality (renewable target, CHOGM hosting, vulnerable-citizen framing). Anchor 3.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to published 50.0. No math-hygiene flag. Sub-threshold delta. First agent baseline confirms functional-band placement at lower bound (10 points above critical floor at 40).

negativeSignals: Women's parliamentary representation gap (6 of 52 seats) despite first-female-PM context; matai (chiefly) governance gender-asymmetry legacy; small-island-state structural climate-displacement existential risk; 2021 constitutional crisis legacy (resolved but recent).
positiveSignals: Fiame Naomi Mata'afa first female PM elected 2021; ended 40-year continuous government; post-2021-crisis governance stabilised; 100% renewable electrification by 2030 commitment; CHOGM 2024 host; UNGA climate engagement; Pacific COP 2026 solidarity; people-centred-development framing; women's-leadership-quotas advocacy; PIF/CHOGM/SPC/ADB multilateral scaffolding; constitutional democracy operational.
sector_context: Countries — Pacific small-island-developing-state (SIDS) cluster. Cross-reference Palau (also assessed this cycle), Fiji, Vanuatu, Tonga, Federated States of Micronesia. Samoa distinguished by first-female-PM leadership, CHOGM 2024 hosting, and active climate-leadership posture. Rotation backfill baseline confirms functional placement. No in-window structural events disrupt baseline.
open_actions_resolved: First agent baseline established for Samoa at 50.0 / Functional
open_actions_carried: Watch: 2026 Samoa election cycle (Mata'afa government term)
Watch: Pacific COP 2026 outcomes and Samoa positioning
Watch: any climate-displacement event affecting Samoan population
Watch: women's parliamentary representation trajectory (any legally enforced quota legislation)
